summary: sensivity 2.5.52 ships obfuscated server payload and shell-spawning launcher with host enumeration
details: The package ships multiple components consistent with a supply-chain attack rather than a legitimate library. cli.js requires child_process and uses http.get together with shell enumeration commands (ls, ping) and process environment access — the classic shape of a host-recon-and-exfiltration script. launcher.js imports child_process and invokes execSync with powershell at multiple call sites (lines 131, 142, 151, 246), giving the package the ability to run arbitrary PowerShell on Windows hosts at execution time. server.obf.js is a heavily obfuscated single-line bundle using mangled `_0x...` identifiers, which hides the actual behavior from review. The combination of a recon/exfil-shaped CLI, a multi-PowerShell-invocation launcher, and an obfuscated server-side payload is not consistent with any benign use case and presents a direct risk of code execution and data exfiltration on machines that install or run this package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
